commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Tob, to the north of Galaad, of which it is a part. (Josephus) It is called Tubim, 1 Machabees v. See 2 Kings x. And robbers. This is a farther explication of rekim, poor, vain fellows, chap ix. They did not infest the Israelites, but made war on their enemies around; latro, in Latin, often signifies a soldier, particularly such as lived on plunder, as we read in Plautus. ) Latrocinatus annos decem, mercedem accipio. Some have imagined that Jephte was at the head of some banditti. Augustine, q. ) But David's followers were of the same description (1 Kings xxii. 2,) as those of Jephte, men of determined resolution and valour. (Calmet) Such a man as Jephte, was therefore a valuable acquisition to the dispirited Israelites; and Providence had inured him to labour, and endued him with extraordinary prudence, notwithstanding his want of education, ver. Necessity has often supplied every deficiency, and produced the most consummate generals. Hebrew and Septuagint, "and there were gathered unto Jephte vain men, and they went out with him.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
